cand_p1.evidence: H1: trained SAE absorption=0.500 (deterministic across 3 seeds), random baseline=0.147, Cohen's d=8.94. H_Mech: encoder effect=0.191, decoder effect=0.0. H3: steering verified, 1.62x sensitivity ratio. H_Safe: p=0.695 on synthetic SAE (wrong experiment, not Gemma Scope).

h3_steering.evidence: Steering implementation fixed. Absorbed features show 1.62x more sensitivity. But effect is modest; Basu et al. showed zero effect. Needs logit-level metrics and more seeds for publishable causal claim.

h_safe.evidence: Pilot tested synthetic SAE, not Gemma Scope. p=0.695 is not informative about real Gemma Scope SAEs. Requires Gemma Scope installation as prerequisite.

cand_eco.evidence: H2 falsification (rho=+0.171 wrong direction) undermines competitive exclusion theoretical foundation. Deferred.

cand_geom.evidence: Training-free 15-min experiment. AUC > 0.75 is a high bar. Partial overlap with OrtSAE geometry approach.

cand_ens.evidence: 2-hour training cost. Partial overlap with Gadgil et al. 50% recovery threshold is aggressive. Lowest priority.

reasons: H1 confirmed with deterministic result: trained SAE absorption=0.500 across all seeds vs random baseline=0.147 (d=8.94)
H_Mech MAJOR finding: absorption is entirely ENCODER-driven (effect=0.191), decoder geometry contributes ZERO
H3 steering now works: absorbed features show 1.62x higher sensitivity to steering
H_Safe pilot tested synthetic SAE (p=0.695) -- wrong experiment; Gemma Scope needed but prerequisite not met
Clear path to full experiment: multi-seed factorial + real SAEs + logit-level steering + Gemma Scope retry
Resource efficient: all individual experiments within 1-hour budget

critical_findings.finding: Absorption is encoder-driven, NOT decoder geometry
critical_findings.evidence: H_Mech factorial: A=C (0.299=0.299), B=D (0.490=0.484). Decoder geometry effect = 0.0. Encoder alignment effect = 0.191.
critical_findings.implication: Paper narrative must change: absorption comes from encoder learning hierarchical representations, not from decoder structure

critical_findings.evidence: All 3 seeds (42,43,44) show exactly 0.500 absorption. Zero variance.
critical_findings.implication: Absorption is a fixed property of trained SAE geometry, not stochastic

critical_findings.evidence: delta_norm > 0 for all alpha > 0. Absorbed features 1.62x more sensitive to steering.
critical_findings.implication: Can now make causal claims about absorption impact via steering

critical_findings.evidence: p=0.695 on synthetic SAE. Gemma Scope SAEs were not available.
critical_findings.implication: H_Safe hypothesis is NOT falsified. Need Gemma Scope installation to properly test.

untested_hypotheses: H_Safe on real Gemma Scope SAEs
next_actions: Full multi-seed factorial: 5 seeds x 4 conditions x 200+ samples to confirm encoder effect reproducibility
Update paper narrative: absorption is encoder-driven, not decoder geometry
Attempt Gemma Scope SAEs installation via sae-lens
Retry H_Safe on Gemma Scope SAEs if installation succeeds
Logit-level steering validation: switch from feature activation to logit-level metrics per Basu et al.
Document H2 as honest negative result (wrong direction frequency correlation)

negative_results.interpretation: Absorption positively correlates with feature activation frequency, opposite of competitive exclusion prediction.
WRONG EXPERIMENT. Synthetic SAE not informative about Gemma Scope. H_Safe NOT falsified.
